tool_snippets.snippet: ### Step 8: `execute_srd46_sql` — sql_query: SELECT ... FROM ligand_card ... JOIN ligand_pka_measured ...
sql_query: "SELECT lc.ligand_id, lc.ligand_name_SRD, lc.formula, COUNT(*) as pka_count ... HAVING COUNT(*) > 6 ORDER BY pka_count DESC LIMIT 30"

Output shows 25 rows then: "... 5 more row(s) omitted"; query filters COUNT(*) > 6 and ORDER BY pka_count DESC, so remaining omitted entries are additional ligands with pKa_count = 7.

tool_snippets.snippet: SELECT ... COUNT(*) as pka_count ... HAVING COUNT(*) > 6 ORDER BY pka_count DESC LIMIT 30
last shown rows have pka_count = 7 (e.g., ligand_7250 ... | 7), implying omitted rows are also 7 given descending order.

paragraphs.text:
### Chemistry Insights

- **Large polyamine macrocycles dominate the top spots.** [33]aneN11 and [36]aneN12 — cyclic rings bearing 11 and 12 nitrogen donors — each show 11 pKa steps, reflecting sequential protonation of every amine nitrogen. The large ring size allows all nitrogens to be protonated without excessive electrostatic repulsion.

- **Polyaminocarboxylate and phosphonate chelators** (DTPA, TTHA, PHOA, DTPP) rank highly because they combine multiple amine and carboxylate/phosphonate groups, each contributing one or more ionizable protons. These are widely used in medical imaging (MRI contrast agents) and nuclear medicine.

- **Phytic acid** (myo-inositol hexaphosphate, 8 pKa values) is a biologically important molecule found in seeds and grains; its many phosphate groups give it exceptional metal-chelating ability, which is why it is considered an antinutrient that sequesters dietary minerals like Zn²⁺ and Fe³⁺.

> **Source:** NIST SRD-46 (Critically Selected Stability Constants of Metal Complexes Database)

Mechanistic rationale (sequential protonation of amines) is not stated in SRD-46 rows; it is standard acid–base chemistry for polyamines.
Effect of macrocycle size on protonation/electrostatic repulsion is chemical reasoning not given in SRD-46 data rows.

Query orders by pka_count DESC; PHOA (10), DTPA (8), TTHA (8), DTPP (8) appear in the returned top list.

tool_snippets.snippet: HAVING COUNT(*) > 6 ORDER BY pka_count DESC LIMIT 30

grounded_claims.evidence_snippet: Names indicate amine “nitrilo” plus carboxylate/phosphonate: ...pentaacetic acid; ...hexaacetic acid; ...octaacetic acid; ...phosphonic acid.
High pka_count values imply multiple ionizable protons (PHOA 10; DTPA/TTHA/DTPP 8).
SRD-46 tool results list thermodynamic/pKa data only; no application/usage info (MRI, nuclear medicine) is provided.
Filler span: no useful database-groundable information.
Biological importance of phytic acid is general knowledge; SRD-46 data here do not cover biological roles.
Occurrence in seeds/grains is general nutritional knowledge, not present in SRD-46 rows.
Chelating ability from multiple phosphates is chemical reasoning; SRD-46 rows here do not state chelation strength.
“Antinutrient” status is nutritional context not covered by SRD-46 tables.
Sequestration of Zn2+ and Fe3+ by phytic acid is well-known; SRD-46 tool results provided do not include such biological statements.
Tool access is to SRD-46: “## execute_srd46_sql - 30 row(s)” (SRD-46-specific tool).
